next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting the minimum presentation fidelity required before formal external core-fun testing: raw graybox geometry and debug effects; near-final visual and audio presentation; or a readability-complete prototype using coherent temporary art and audio with production-quality telegraphs, hit feedback, weapon differentiation, HUD clarity, reduced-effect controls, and performance instrumentation while deferring final assets and polish. persistence.batching_rule: Checkpoint queued state after several questions for flow; formally persist D-201 through D-210 at D-210 unless an earlier handoff is requested

constraints: Stage 1 must prove movement aiming four-weapon combat enemy readability room traversal quick restart and voluntary repeat-run desire with temporary content
Stage 2 must turn the proven loop into one complete factory level with reliable saves checkpoints loot risk shop strongboxes completion replay scalable effects and diagnostic evidence
Broader content production begins only after both replay desire and a stable extensible production loop are demonstrated
Define evidence and kill criteria before testing rather than moving the goalposts afterward
Exact thresholds and tuning coefficients remain prototype and playtest variables
The developer must first find the prototype compelling enough to replay voluntarily and pursue mastery or better execution
A small external target-player group must then demonstrate genuine repeat-play interest despite temporary visuals and limited progression
Failure at either stage keeps the project in prototype iteration rather than advancing to full vertical-slice production
Tester counts and exact replay thresholds must be declared before each formal test round
Actual replay behavior determines whether the external fun gate passes while interviews and ratings explain why
Observe voluntary restarts alternate loadout trials mastery or record chasing and later-session return interest
Replay must be motivated by enjoyment experimentation or mastery rather than merely helping the developer test
Major frustration must not consistently overwhelm the desire to continue
Thresholds may change between formal rounds with written justification but never retroactively to declare a weak result successful
After a limited predeclared number of failed formal rounds, likely two or three, pause for a written diagnosis
Identify whether failure comes from movement aiming four-weapon readability enemy design level pacing onboarding or replay incentives
Permit one substantial evidence-backed pivot with a fresh predeclared test gate
Stop or shelve the project if the revised prototype still fails
Do not enter full vertical-slice production while the result remains ambiguous
First prove intrinsic movement and combat quality using curated fixed four-weapon loadouts
After the baseline loop is readable and promising add a deliberately tiny nonpersistent randomized weapon or reward wrapper
Use the second pass to test whether discovery and experimentation improve replay desire without hiding weak combat behind extrinsic rewards
Defer the mature economy permanent inventory advanced rarity progression strongboxes shops and save integration to Stage 2
Keep test conditions and loadout identities reproducible for comparison
Maintain one sterile rapidly resettable arena for controlled mechanical comparisons
Also test the same mechanics in a compact interconnected route with several rooms corridors hazards and traversal choices
Use the arena to isolate tuning effects and the mini-route to test navigation pacing route mastery and encounter transitions
Reuse the same temporary enemies weapons and art across both environments
Neither prototype environment must become the final authored weapons-factory level
Build roughly five or six mechanically distinct prototype weapons supporting several meaningful four-weapon combinations
Cover representative automatic heavy projectile spread or burst beam charge or heat homing or utility and deliberately unusual synergy behavior
Temporary art and audio are acceptable while cadence handling power-ammo behavior movement interaction and readability must be representative
Do not require the complete authored six-to-eight-weapon MVP roster before the core-fun gate
Avoid designing the system around only one unusually compatible fixed loadout
Stage 1 includes a direct pursuer or close-pressure enemy a ranged projectile enemy and a positioning or area-denial enemy
Add one tougher elite variant that combines pressure without becoming the full upgraded-droid climax
Use mixed encounters to test target prioritization thruster use projectile readability crowd control weapon synergy and encounter composition
Difficulty variation must not rely only on health inflation
Defer the remaining ordinary factory roles and upgraded-droid climax to Stage 2
